label.name: We start with the first block, please don't miss t...
label.intro:
We start with the first block, please don't miss the mini-video 🔥 and participate virtually in a conversation ☕ about the initial paperwork 😓, the most common difficulties of adaptation to the new context 💌 👩💻 and erasmusapp.eu 🤳 as a common space of orientation for the exchange period.

The conversation helps us to explain how we will accompany 🤹 ️ your exchange from the IRO. At the end of this section you will find a list of FAQs that students usually ask us 'before the exchange'.

We feel connected to you because we have also lived an exchange experience 👵. There is so much we learned in that time 👷️😊 today we know how helpful it is to name those new skills and strategies that later served us in our personal and professional development. This course aims to help you broaden your vision 🕵️️, to help you explore learning that goes beyond academic content.

You will remember this from our first briefing 🏁 Don't you remember? No problem, you can always access the content of the first briefing (pdf) from here.

In this period (before the mobility) you start to develop new skills 😎. We deal with paperwork but at the same time we bring into play our skills in a context of digitalisation 👨💻 and our skills to manage in a multicultural context 🌍.

We have created a pathway to help you become aware of these new skills, but first access the mini-video 🔥 it's important!

If you have already watched the mini video 🔥 the next step is to access the different key questions on cultural diversity.

Finally, get to know our proposal for the development of the personal exploration process 🕵️️ that will help you later on to defend the added value of a professional profile of someone who has participated in an exchange experience, complex, yes, but we assure you that when you have lived it everything makes sense.


Block presentation (voice memo, Spanish): Presentación Bloque 01

 🏁 Content of the first information meeting, Spanish (pdf)

🕵️‍♂️ Proposal for the development of the personal exploration process (mapping your exchange experience)

🌍 Erasmusapp.eu
